title: The Reactive Genome
description:
Take a few deep breaths: Holism in, Reductionism out. 

We need to stop talking about "self/non-self recognition" for three reasons.

First, it sets up a strict self/non-self dichotomy, when the presence of autoimmunity, cancer, and disease remind us that our borders are ever-permeable. Does the immunology community not heed the 1871 warning by A. Rimbaud that "I is another"? Second, "recognition" implies that the immune cell surveyor will re-cognize (to cognize again) the antigen based upon previous encounters, which we know to be false, as antibodies are able to active immune cascades upon their very first introduction to a suitable partner. Lastly, the dyadic interaction is falsely given a sense of intrinsic reactivity, when we know that context is everything: a "self" antigen in an inflamed tissue may be the genesis of a lifelong chronic autoimmune reaction, while if I were to detail the shocking kaleidoscope of immunological tolerance to "non-self" that is constantly occurring in the gut, you would blush. 

To obviate these concerns, I here suggest the term "self/non-self precognition". This term squarely applies to normative immune system function, but has zippers and stretchy bits that allow it to expand itself into the domains of autoimmunity, cancer, pregnancy, and health. Precognition is the idea that the lived ecological experiences of an organism or cell bear upon its future decisions -- an idea so outrageously obvious that I am unsurprised to see it categorically dismissed by the intellectual orthodoxy. The context which an immune cell finds itself (e.g. navigating the stressful jungle of an inflamed pro-tumor, or enjoying the late-1960's-like vibe of the placenta) will dictate its response to a given encounter, not the molecular identity of the binding partner. 

In short: The Secret Scientific Brotherhood of the Immuno-nati uses the word "recognition" to pithily describe the immune system, a term that is full to the gills with misconception. I provide the alternative term "precognition", which by adding but a single letter to the "concept emeritus" of recognition, points the way towards a whole new research program in the multilevel evolutionary ecology of immunity.
